title: Counterfactual rejoin-pressure and opponent-response features
summary: Recent F1 strategy papers emphasize that pit decisions depend on competitors’ actions, not just self tyre state.
rationale: Recent F1 strategy papers emphasize that pit decisions depend on competitors’ actions, not just self tyre state. A more concrete testable version is to approximate whether pitting now or next lap would rejoin into traffic or gain undercut leverage on nearby rivals.
implementation_hint: Within each Race_Year x Race x LapNumber, reconstruct the field snapshot and derive traffic proxies: nearby cars by position and pace residual, number of rivals in the likely rejoin band, local clean-air score, undercut-target count, and whether a one-lap delay changes the rejoin band. Feed these into a tree model or a small specialist blended with the base model.
expected_effect: Adds signal in mid-race battles where identical tyre states imply different stop decisions because traffic cost differs.
risk: Without exact gaps these are proxies, so noisy field reconstruction can hurt; prefer coarse bands and OOF blending over hard rules.
